generated_requirements.text: The system shall continuously read temperature data from thermostats installed in each of the twelve zones across the three-floor office building.
generated_requirements.rationale: Continuous temperature monitoring is the core sensing function of THEMAS.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support exactly twelve thermostat zones distributed across three floors of the office building.
generated_requirements.rationale: The physical building layout defines the scope of zone management the system must handle.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compare each zone's current temperature reading against its configured target temperature setting to determine whether heating or cooling action is required.
generated_requirements.rationale: Temperature comparison against configured settings drives all heating and cooling control decisions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall compare each zone's current temperature reading against configured trigger thresholds to determine when to activate or deactivate heating and cooling units.
generated_requirements.rationale: Trigger thresholds define the precise activation and deactivation points for HVAC units.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically activate a heating or cooling unit for a zone when the zone's temperature crosses the configured trigger threshold.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic unit activation is the primary control output of the thermostat management system.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically deactivate a heating or cooling unit for a zone when the zone's temperature returns within the acceptable range relative to the configured settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic deactivation prevents energy waste once a zone reaches its target temperature.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a configurable maximum limit on the number of heating and cooling units that may operate simultaneously across the building.
generated_requirements.rationale: A simultaneous unit limit prevents electrical overload and manages energy consumption.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall queue activation requests for heating or cooling units that cannot be fulfilled due to the simultaneous unit limit being reached, and process them in order as capacity becomes available.
generated_requirements.rationale: A request queue ensures overflow activation requests are handled fairly and systematically.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log a denied request event to the database whenever a unit activation request cannot be immediately fulfilled due to the simultaneous unit limit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Logging denied requests provides an audit trail for capacity management analysis.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall trigger a distinct audible alarm on the supervisor's workstation when a temperature reading falls below the defined valid minimum range for a zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: An audible alarm for out-of-range low readings prompts immediate supervisor attention to potential faults.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall trigger a distinct audible alarm on the supervisor's workstation when a temperature reading exceeds the defined valid maximum range for a zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: An audible alarm for out-of-range high readings alerts the supervisor to potentially dangerous conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall trigger a distinct audible alarm on the supervisor's workstation when a zone's temperature reading deviates beyond a configured deviation limit from the target setting.
generated_requirements.rationale: Deviation alarms distinguish excessive drift from simple out-of-range faults, enabling targeted responses.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall produce acoustically distinguishable alarm signals for each distinct alarm condition type (invalid range low, invalid range high, deviation exceeded) on the supervisor's workstation.
generated_requirements.rationale: Distinct alarm sounds allow the supervisor to identify the alarm type without viewing the screen.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a dedicated supervisor interface through which the building supervisor can view the current temperature and status of all twelve zones.
generated_requirements.rationale: A consolidated status view enables the supervisor to monitor the entire building at a glance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to adjust the target temperature setting for any individual zone through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable per-zone temperature settings accommodate varying occupancy and comfort requirements.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to manually reset any active alarm through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual alarm reset gives the supervisor control to acknowledge and clear alarms after investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to manually turn on or off any individual heating unit through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual heating unit control allows the supervisor to override automatic decisions when needed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to manually turn on or off any individual cooling unit through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Manual cooling unit control allows the supervisor to override automatic decisions when needed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all heating and cooling unit status change events (activation and deactivation) to the Microsoft Access database, including the zone identifier, unit type, timestamp, and trigger source (automatic or manual).
generated_requirements.rationale: Comprehensive unit status logging supports operational history reporting and accountability.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all alarm occurrence events to the Microsoft Access database, including the zone identifier, alarm type, temperature reading at time of alarm, and timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Alarm event logging enables post-incident analysis and trend identification.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all alarm reset events to the Microsoft Access database, including the alarm type, zone identifier, timestamp, and whether the reset was manual or automatic.
generated_requirements.rationale: Logging alarm resets provides a complete alarm lifecycle record for audit purposes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to generate an operational history report from the Microsoft Access database, displaying logged events within a user-specified date and time range.
generated_requirements.rationale: Operational history reports give the supervisor visibility into past system activity for review and compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to generate a monthly statistical summary report from the Microsoft Access database, including aggregated metrics such as unit run times, alarm frequencies, and denied request counts per zone.
generated_requirements.rationale: Monthly summaries support capacity planning, energy management, and maintenance scheduling.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to configure the maximum simultaneous unit limit through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: A configurable limit allows the supervisor to adapt the system to changing electrical or operational constraints.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to configure the valid temperature range (minimum and maximum) for each zone through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Per-zone valid range configuration accommodates different environmental requirements across zones.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow the building supervisor to configure the temperature deviation limit for each zone through the supervisor interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configurable deviation limits allow fine-tuning of alarm sensitivity per zone.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall poll all twelve zone thermostats for temperature readings at a configurable interval not exceeding 60 seconds.
generated_requirements.rationale: A maximum 60-second polling interval ensures temperature changes are detected and acted upon in near real-time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall activate or deactivate a heating or cooling unit within 5 seconds of determining that the trigger threshold has been crossed.
generated_requirements.rationale: A fast response time ensures the building environment is controlled promptly after a threshold event.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall sound the audible alarm on the supervisor's workstation within 3 seconds of detecting an out-of-range or deviation-exceeded condition.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid alarm notification minimises the time before the supervisor can respond to a fault condition.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall have an operational availability of at least 99.5% during normal building operating hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability ensures continuous environmental monitoring and control without significant downtime.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from an unplanned software failure and resume normal monitoring and control operations within 2 minutes without requiring manual intervention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Automatic recovery minimises the window during which the building is unmonitored after a crash.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall not lose any logged event data in the Microsoft Access database in the event of an unplanned software or hardware failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data integrity of the event log is essential for accurate reporting and audit trails.

generated_requirements.text: The supervisor interface shall require authenticated login with a unique username and password before granting access to any system controls or configuration settings.
generated_requirements.rationale: Authentication prevents unauthorised personnel from altering thermostat settings or overriding unit controls.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce role-based access control so that only authorised supervisor accounts can modify configuration settings, while read-only accounts may only view status and reports.
generated_requirements.rationale: Role-based access limits the risk of accidental or malicious misconfiguration by non-supervisory users.

generated_requirements.text: All communication between the THEMAS software and the thermostat hardware interfaces shall be validated to reject malformed or out-of-range data before processing.
generated_requirements.rationale: Input validation prevents corrupted or spoofed sensor data from causing incorrect control actions.

generated_requirements.text: The supervisor interface shall display clear, plain-language labels and status indicators for all zones, units, and alarms, requiring no specialised technical training to interpret.
generated_requirements.rationale: An intuitive interface reduces operator error and allows the supervisor to act quickly in alarm situations.

generated_requirements.text: The supervisor interface shall provide confirmation prompts before executing irreversible or high-impact actions, such as manually shutting down a heating or cooling unit.
generated_requirements.rationale: Confirmation prompts reduce the risk of accidental commands that could disrupt building comfort.

generated_requirements.text: A new building supervisor with no prior THEMAS experience shall be able to perform all primary tasks (view status, adjust settings, reset alarms, generate reports) after no more than 2 hours of training.
generated_requirements.rationale: Low training overhead ensures operational continuity when supervisor personnel change.

generated_requirements.text: The system's source code and architecture shall be documented sufficiently to allow a developer unfamiliar with the system to understand, modify, and extend any module within 5 business days.
generated_requirements.rationale: Good documentation reduces the cost and risk of future maintenance and feature additions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed with modular components so that the number of zones or floors can be increased without requiring architectural redesign, only configuration or minor code changes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular design future-proofs the system against building expansions or reconfigurations.

generated_requirements.text: The thermostat hardware interface layer shall be abstracted behind a defined API so that alternative thermostat hardware models can be integrated by replacing only the interface module.
generated_requirements.rationale: Hardware abstraction reduces the effort required to support different or upgraded thermostat hardware.

generated_requirements.text: The system's database layer shall be abstracted so that the Microsoft Access database can be replaced with an alternative database engine with changes confined to the data access module only.
generated_requirements.rationale: Database abstraction protects the system from vendor lock-in and simplifies future database migrations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all configuration changes made through the supervisor interface to the Microsoft Access database, recording the parameter changed, old value, new value, user, and timestamp.
generated_requirements.rationale: Configuration change logging provides an audit trail to trace the cause of unexpected system behaviour.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current active alarm status for each zone on the supervisor interface in real time, updating within one polling cycle of the alarm condition being detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Real-time alarm display ensures the supervisor always has an accurate view of the building's alarm state.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall display the current on/off status of each heating and cooling unit on the supervisor interface, updated within one polling cycle of any status change.
generated_requirements.rationale: Up-to-date unit status display allows the supervisor to verify that control actions have taken effect.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain the activation request queue in a persistent manner so that queued requests are not lost in the event of a brief system interruption.
generated_requirements.rationale: Queue persistence ensures no zone is left unserviced due to a transient system fault.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall generate and display reports within 10 seconds of the supervisor submitting a report request, for data sets covering up to 12 months of logged events.
generated_requirements.rationale: Acceptable report generation time ensures the supervisor is not left waiting during operational reviews.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall prevent a zone's heating unit and cooling unit from being active simultaneously, and shall log an error if such a conflict is detected.
generated_requirements.rationale: Simultaneous heating and cooling in the same zone wastes energy and can damage HVAC equipment.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall retain all logged event and configuration change data in the Microsoft Access database for a minimum of 24 months before archiving or deletion.
generated_requirements.rationale: A 24-month retention period ensures sufficient historical data is available for trend analysis and compliance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide an export function allowing the supervisor to export operational history and monthly statistical reports in CSV format for use in external tools.
generated_requirements.rationale: CSV export enables the supervisor to perform further analysis or share reports using standard office tools.

generated_requirements.rationale: Forward-deployed units operating under degraded communications must still be able to contribute XML resources.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow producers to assemble composite XML schemas from existing registered XML tag structures and artifacts.
generated_requirements.rationale: Producers need to reuse and compose standardized components to build new schemas efficiently.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide consumers with a web-based interface to browse the XML tag registry by category, domain, and naming convention.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consumers must be able to discover available XML tag structures through intuitive navigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide consumers with a full-text and metadata search capability to locate XML tags, schemas, and documents within the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Search functionality is essential for consumers to efficiently find relevant XML resources across a large registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to validate XML documents against registered schemas through the web-based registry interface.
generated_requirements.rationale: Validation ensures that consumer-produced XML documents conform to standardized tag structures before use.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow consumers to download XML tags, schemas, and documents from the registry for use in their own applications.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consumers must be able to retrieve and reuse standardized XML resources in their operational systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with the ability to define, enforce, and modify XML naming conventions across the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: Consistent naming conventions are critical for semantic interoperability across the defense information enterprise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with the ability to create, modify, suspend, and delete user accounts and assign roles (producer, consumer, manager).
generated_requirements.rationale: User permission governance is required to control access and maintain registry integrity.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Discretionary Access Control (DAC) to restrict read and write access to XML registry resources based on user identity and ownership.
generated_requirements.rationale: DAC is required to protect sensitive XML resources and ensure only authorized users can access or modify them.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce Mandatory Access Control (MAC) by assigning security labels to XML resources and users, preventing access that violates the defined security policy.
generated_requirements.rationale: MAC is required for defense environments where classified data must be strictly compartmentalized.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall integrate with COTS Relational Database Management System (RDBMS) products for persistent storage of XML registry data and metadata.
generated_requirements.rationale: Integration with existing defense database infrastructure avoids redundant investment and leverages proven storage solutions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall expose standard APIs (e.g., SOAP, REST, or equivalent) to enable heterogeneous platforms and applications to interact with the XML registry programmatically.
generated_requirements.rationale: Standard APIs are necessary to support data management and interoperability across diverse defense platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support data replication of the XML registry across geographically distributed nodes to ensure availability in disconnected or degraded network environments.
generated_requirements.rationale: Replication is essential for forward-deployed units that cannot rely on continuous connectivity to a central registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support distributed transaction management to maintain data consistency across replicated registry nodes during concurrent updates.
generated_requirements.rationale: Transaction management prevents data corruption and ensures registry integrity when multiple nodes are updated simultaneously.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support COE (Common Operating Environment) compliance, conforming to applicable DII COE standards and interfaces.
generated_requirements.rationale: COE compliance is mandated for integration within the Defense Information Infrastructure.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain a versioned audit log of all registry changes, including submissions, modifications, deletions, and access control changes, with timestamps and user identifiers.
generated_requirements.rationale: Audit logs are required for accountability, forensic analysis, and registry integrity governance.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support multiple operational modes including peacetime training, maintenance, crisis, and wartime operations, adjusting resource prioritization and access policies accordingly.
generated_requirements.rationale: The system must adapt its behavior across the full spectrum of military readiness conditions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide managers with a registry integrity monitoring dashboard that reports on schema consistency, naming convention violations, and replication status.
generated_requirements.rationale: Managers need visibility into registry health to proactively identify and resolve integrity issues.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support conflict detection and resolution workflows when replicated registry nodes contain divergent versions of the same XML artifact.
generated_requirements.rationale: Conflict resolution is necessary to maintain a single authoritative version of each XML resource after network partitions are healed.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall allow managers to approve or reject producer submissions before they are published to the registry.
generated_requirements.rationale: An approval workflow ensures that only validated and compliant XML resources are made available to consumers.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide producers with schema validation feedback at submission time, identifying structural and naming convention errors before registry ingestion.
generated_requirements.rationale: Early validation reduces the burden on managers and improves the quality of submitted XML artifacts.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support semantic tagging and classification of XML resources using a controlled vocabulary aligned with defense domain taxonomies.
generated_requirements.rationale: Semantic classification enables meaningful discovery and reuse of XML resources across diverse defense applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide notification mechanisms (e.g., email or system alerts) to inform producers and consumers of registry changes relevant to their subscribed resources.
generated_requirements.rationale: Notifications keep stakeholders informed of updates that may affect their dependent applications.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall respond to consumer search queries within 3 seconds under normal operational load on a standard defense network connection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely search responses are critical for operational efficiency, especially during crisis and wartime operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support a minimum of 500 concurrent users across producer, consumer, and manager roles without degradation of response times beyond defined thresholds.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defense organizations involve large numbers of simultaneous users across installations and deployed units.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall achieve an availability of at least 99.9% uptime for the central registry during peacetime operations, measured on a monthly basis.
generated_requirements.rationale: High availability is required to ensure continuous access to XML resources for defense operations.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain full operational capability for locally replicated registry nodes even when disconnected from the central registry for up to 72 hours.
generated_requirements.rationale: Forward-deployed units must sustain operations under prolonged degraded communications conditions.

generated_requirements.text: All data transmitted between registry nodes and between clients and the registry shall be encrypted using FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic algorithms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of data in transit is mandatory to protect sensitive defense information from interception.

generated_requirements.text: All XML registry data at rest shall be encrypted using FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic algorithms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Encryption of stored data protects classified and sensitive XML resources from unauthorized physical access.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall authenticate all users via multi-factor authentication (MFA) before granting access to any registry function.
generated_requirements.rationale: MFA is required in defense environments to prevent unauthorized access through compromised credentials.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall automatically terminate idle user sessions after a configurable inactivity timeout not exceeding 15 minutes.
generated_requirements.rationale: Session timeout reduces the risk of unauthorized access through unattended authenticated sessions.

generated_requirements.text: The system's web-based interface shall conform to Section 508 accessibility standards to ensure usability by personnel with disabilities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Federal accessibility requirements mandate Section 508 compliance for defense information systems.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide context-sensitive help documentation and tooltips within the web-based interface to guide producers, consumers, and managers through all major workflows.
generated_requirements.rationale: Inline guidance reduces training burden and user error in a complex multi-role registry system.

generated_requirements.text: A new user with basic XML knowledge shall be able to complete a registry search and download workflow without formal training, within 10 minutes of first use.
generated_requirements.rationale: Ease of use is critical for consumers who may be deployed personnel with limited time for system familiarization.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall recover from a single node failure and restore full service within 5 minutes through automated failover mechanisms.
generated_requirements.rationale: Rapid automated recovery is essential to minimize operational disruption in defense environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall maintain data integrity such that no committed registry transaction is lost in the event of a system crash or power failure.
generated_requirements.rationale: Data loss in the XML registry could compromise semantic interoperability across the defense enterprise.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be designed using a modular architecture that allows individual components (e.g., search engine, validation engine, replication service) to be updated or replaced independently.
generated_requirements.rationale: Modular design reduces the cost and risk of future upgrades and technology refreshes.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide comprehensive developer documentation, including API specifications, data models, and deployment guides, to support maintenance and integration activities.
generated_requirements.rationale: Thorough documentation is essential for long-term system sustainment by defense maintenance teams.

generated_requirements.text: The system codebase shall achieve a minimum unit test coverage of 80% to facilitate safe modification and regression detection.
generated_requirements.rationale: Adequate test coverage reduces the risk of introducing defects during system updates and patches.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be deployable on multiple operating system platforms including Windows Server and major Linux distributions without modification to the core application code.
generated_requirements.rationale: Defense environments operate heterogeneous infrastructure, requiring the system to run across multiple OS platforms.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall be compatible with at least two COTS RDBMS products (e.g., Oracle and Microsoft SQL Server) through a database abstraction layer.
generated_requirements.rationale: A database abstraction layer prevents vendor lock-in and supports deployment across varied defense database environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support deployment in containerized environments (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to facilitate rapid provisioning across fixed and tactical installations.
generated_requirements.rationale: Containerization enables consistent and rapid deployment across diverse defense computing environments.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall scale horizontally to accommodate registry growth of up to 10 million XML artifacts without architectural redesign.
generated_requirements.rationale: The defense information enterprise generates large volumes of XML resources that the registry must accommodate over time.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall complete synchronization of offline-queued submissions to the central registry within 10 minutes of network connectivity being restored.
generated_requirements.rationale: Timely synchronization ensures that offline contributions are rapidly integrated into the authoritative registry.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall log all security-relevant events (authentication attempts, access control decisions, privilege changes) to a tamper-evident audit store.
generated_requirements.rationale: Tamper-evident security logging is required for compliance with defense security policies and incident investigation.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall enforce a maximum XML artifact file size limit of 100 MB per submission to prevent resource exhaustion attacks and storage abuse.
generated_requirements.rationale: File size limits protect the registry infrastructure from denial-of-service conditions caused by oversized submissions.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall provide a mean time between failures (MTBF) of no less than 2,000 hours of continuous operation under normal load.
generated_requirements.rationale: A high MTBF ensures the system can sustain extended operational periods without unplanned outages.

generated_requirements.text: The system shall support role-based access control (RBAC) such that permissions are assigned to roles (producer, consumer, manager) and users inherit permissions through role membership.
generated_requirements.rationale: RBAC simplifies permission management and ensures consistent enforcement of access policies across all user classes.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Environmental Mode: This is the real-time operations that must react to varying degrees of readiness to full scale wartime operations such as crisis planning with the use of heterogeneous data types and sources, transfer capabilities, data management services. COE Compliance. XML Services shall be segmented. COE sponsors shall adhere to compliance level requirements described in the I&RTS. In the fixed (static) mode of operation (base or data processing megacenter), the data management services shall have the capability of being tuned by on-site personnel to adjust for varying workloads and sizes of associated databases. These workloads and databases are expected to change more frequently and to a greater extent than for processing associated with deployed units In a changing (dynamic) environment, such as with deployed units, the workload and database sizes may be more predetermined (given a more precise mission) and require access to fewer data management administrative capabilities than needed in a fixed environment. The XS shall have the ability to redefine or reset names of connect descriptors to database server instances. Connect descriptors are fully qualified object names and include address (protocol/host/port) and instance name. In a degraded communications environment, there is a need, for example, to be able to reset session time-out values if the data management services are being accessed by users affected by the communications degradation. At a minimum, the session time-out values shall be user definable and be able to be reset prior to initialization of a user session. The goal is to provide the option of dynamically changing session time values based on current communications performance identified by capabilities of the network management or DBMS. End User Mode: Portion of XS services shall be used by various classes of users: data consumers, data and database managers, network information infrastructure resource managers. Some of these uses of the data management services will entail unique requirements that shall be fulfilled within the capability of XS services. Maintenance Mode: This mode includes modification and/or addition of application data segments, user permission, privileges, and restructuring storage and memory areas. In addition, maintenance also shall pertain to shutdown, open not-mounted and online/off-line implementations, modifications, upgrade, or other related actions. The data management services shall support managing various types of data, database architectures and platforms that includes hardware and software at the specified sites. Training Mode. In support of training activities, the data management services shall provide for the same processing as would be encountered in a production environment. However, access to the database may be via a training application access to the DBMS rather than from the production mission application.

ground_truth_requirements.text: Producer services A producer is an agent that contributes metadata for inclusion into an XML Registry for the purposes of ensuring maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document. To contribute metadata to a registry, the producer must be able to receive XML registry forms, submit metadata and the related information resource artifacts, and notify. Produce and display submittal forms as part of the Information Resource Submittal Package from the web containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. The Package is to be compressed and emailed or sent ftp to an addressee. Download Information Resource Submittal Package from the web containing forms, instructions, and tools for submission to XML Registry. Submit prescribed metadata related to information resource type, information resource association, status code, data types specified and other related information specified in Appendix A within a combination of forms. These forms are part of the Information Resource Submittal Package containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. The Package is to be compressed and emailed or sent ftp to an addressee. Submit metadata by an on-line interactive process Submit metadata by a off-line and interactive batch process Parse submitted XML Registry specification forms Populate XML Registry database Modify of specified terms & definitions of metadata and status of Information Resources. Associate Information Resources specified in Appendix A. Annotate rejected? Assemble registered information resources to form new components. Assemble new DTDs from current Produce DTD as an instantiation (others are database schema, message definition) for modeling environment. Notify change in Information Resources or authorized producer of tag. A consumer examines a registry to select a tag structure for reuse in one or more applications that will exchange data according to a pre-defined agreement. Versioning relationship: Provide the capability to view an Information resource’s versions Reference Sets: Provide the capability to view text related to the domai values or the related reference set Amplifying information: Provide the capability to view other information resources such as ERwin models, DTDs, documents, etc., which describe or otherwise provide amplifying information. View the XML Tags via a tree/hierarchy structure or tabular format. View the XML Tags by giving the user multiple search options to find a specific Tag. View the XML tags by giving the user the search option to find all tags of a given subscriber/author. Each Information Resource will have its own web page to allow the user to view all pertinent information, according to its information resource type. View the Information Resource Submittal Package containing the following Information Resource artifacts: XML Tag Specification, XML Spec (i.e. DTD, DCD etc.), Sample of XML document of the tag to be submitted. Display an XML Tag Specification form to the author of a given information resource. This XML Tag Spec will be used for inputting the requesting information for a specific Information Resource. Provide capabilities to download the XML Tags or other selected Information Resources. Catalog entities and attributes within servers to enable browsing, searching, retrieving of data related to XML sources. Process ANSI standard SQL as specified in FIPS PUB 127-2 Establish rules to ensure maximum semantic understanding of a term as it appears in an XML document Links to DTDs, DCDs, Namespace Provide a capability to subscribe for notification of changes to Communities of Interest (COE) or Information Resources. Manager services create and manage usernames, superusers Establish acceptable naming convention not to be in conflict with the DOD data standards convention and establish a relationship to other naming conventions. Create a naming structure within the COE architecture to express the context and relationship of the naming convention to other naming conventions specified in the I&RTS. Define a set of metadata tags, information attributed to metadata tags (meta-metadata) and other related terms for the maintenance and control of XML tags. Review/approve submission Monitor changes to data models recorded in Registry pass request off to … (per explicit federation agreements) Design the schema for an XML document Structure services Record structure Check against standard (e.g., style guide wizard for tags) Validate Use and reference existing tags and associated semantic structures Content services Use & reference existing tags & associated semantic structures (extracted from Registry) create new tags & associated semantic structures interchange w/other design tools & environments Record semantic structure in JTA-compliant formalism Generate standard views Version metadata objects Generate xml & schema documents Generate a document that includes (by ref or by value) a schema Develop scheme to permit dynamic cross-referencing and indexing of XML objects. Generate language (natural language) and charset values Check conformance Well-formed Validate Extract views of data Disassemble document to materialize different query results into different tree structure Render Interrogate document Transmit sufficient metadata to construct alternate, semantically-valid views (e.g., XSL) Express version Consume xml & schema documents Parsers (dom, sax) Check conformance Well-formed Validate Create validation constraints shall enable a meaningful sharing of XML-based schemas and related information. Extract semantically-valid views from an XML document (e.g., XSL) Disassemble hierarchical view to relational representation Render

ground_truth_requirements.text: COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). COE Software will be qualified through formal validation tests of the SRS level requirements. The Qualification Methods applied to the software shall include test, demonstration, analysis, and inspection (T, D, A, I). TEST. A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.) and that relies on the collection and subsequent examination of data. DEMONSTRATIONS A qualification method that is carried out by operation of the item/component/I/F (or some part of the computer S/W configuration item, etc.), and that relies on observable functional operation not requiring the use of elaborate instrumentation or special test equipment. ANALYSIS A qualification method that is carried out by the processing of accumulated data. An example of accumulated data is the compilation of data obtained from other qualification methods. Examples of the processing of accumulated data are interpretations or extrapolations made from the data. INSPECTION A qualification method that is carried out by visual examination, physical manipulation, or measurement to verify that the requirements have been satisfied

ground_truth_requirements.text: Automatically: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent on information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information / parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Business Rule: A narrative description of policies, procedures, or principles within an organization. Business rules can be divided in to four categories: definitions, facts, constraints, and derivations. Definitions are business rules that define entities and attributes. Facts are either links (relationships) between entities or associations between an entity and attributes Constraints are conditions about the data that must always be true. They are the integrity rules that protect the data in the eventual database. Derivations are business rules that materialize a new piece of information (often attribute values) from other pieces of information. For example, a mathematical derivation might specify that you can obtain a person's age by subtracting his or her birth date from the current date. Commit/Rollback: An individual transaction is processed (commit) or discarded (rollback) by the proponent maintainer of the data items involved. Discretionary Access Controls (DAC): A means of restricting access to objects based on the identity of subjects or groups to which they belong. The controls are discretionary in the sense that a subject with a certain access permission is capable of passing that permission on to any other subject. Dynamically Generated Processing: Indicates processing initiated during execution of other processes, but dependent upon information and/or parameters to be generated or supplied to these other processes. The information/parameters may be data dependent, or application dependent, or dependent on a manual process/human intervention. It will include controls qualifying the processing involved. Location Transparency: occurs when the physical location of data is transparent to the applications and users of the database system. For example, a view that joins table data from several databases provides location transparency because the user of the view does not need to know where the data originates from. Mandatory Access Control (MAC): Imediates access to an object based on the clearance level of the subject (user) and the sensitivity label of the object. (These controls are always enforced above any discretionary control implemented by users). Mirrored Databases: Replication and maintenance of a database on a transaction basis for the purpose of rapid error or failure recovery as supported by the resident COTS RDBMS own system utilities and operating system. Object: A passive entity that contains or receives information. Access to an object potentially implies access to the information it contains. Examples of objects are records, blocks, pages, segments, files, directories, directory trees, and programs, as well as bits, bytes, words, fields, processors, video displays, keyboards, clocks, printers, and network nodes. Proponent Scheme: Describes the sites at which databases are replicated and also who owns and has update authority with respect to the data at each site. It refers to proponency at the source and record level. Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks (RAID): A RAID system appears as one very large, reliable disk to the CPU. The main reason for using RAID storage is its reliability. RAID has the same advantages as shadowing and striping at a lower cost. There are several types/levels of RAID implementations, including: RAID 0 (known as disk striping), RAID 1 (known as disk shadowing), RAID 3 (in which data is distributed in small increments across all data disks and adds a parity value to a separate disk for recovery if any disk fails, RAID 4 (in which data is distributed in large chunks across all data disks and also has a single parity disk. RAID 4 intended to overcome performance penalties of RAID 3 for small transfers. RAID 5 (in which parity over RAID 3 or RAID 4 implementations), and RAID 6 ( in which two parity disks in addition to data disks are used in an attempt to further improve performance). In a RAID 5 implementation, the data is stored as are check sums and other information about the contents of each disk in the array. If one disk is lost, the others can use the check sums and other stored information to recreate the lost data. Storage system vendors may provide additional enhancements to RAID level implementations to improve performance and reliability. Remote Data Access (RDA): is an ISO (9579) application layer interoperability standard (protocol and formats) to support access by an application to a (remote) DBMSs over an OSI network. The goal of RDA is to allow interoperability between applications (clients) and databases (servers) of different manufacturers so that an application is able to read and update data in remote databases via well defined standards. RDA defines a set of client and server standards and a mapping of SQL commands to these services. RDA also defines an interface to ISO (transaction processing) two phase commit TP services in the case where updates to multiple remote databases need to be coordinated. RDA does not yet define interoperability between server databases (i.e. it is not yet a standard for distributed database management). Replication Scheme: Information that precisely identifies DBs, or partitions of DBs, to be copied and/or distributed, replication schedules, and master/remote sites that are to receive the copies. Spatial DBMS: Geographic information system that organizes and maintains spatial data (i.e. data with graphical attributes) in terms of type, scale, location(s), extent, topology and geometry. Supports queries of spatial data where the selection criteria are defined by spatial attributes. SRI: A Standing Request For Information (SRI) is a capability in which CASS monitors for the occurrence of conditions established by an application program, and notifies the calling or establishing application program when the conditions are satisfied. An SRI may be one of three types: timer-based, data-based, or message-based. Subject: An active entity, generally in the form of a person, process, or device that causes information to flow among objects or changes the system state. Technically, a process/domain pair. Transaction Journalling: Individual messages or database transactions are stored in a journal file, which may be a linear log file or a circular file.

ground_truth_requirements.text: If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element. Example: CTRY_CD, CTRY_NM, CTRY_ABBRD_NM, CTRY_OFF_NM, CTRY_SCP_NT_TX, and CTRY_PSTL_NM are all characteristics (ER-attributes) for the entity CTRY. In the relational world, they are columns within the table ctry, attributes of the entity ctry in er-modeling, and member attributes in object modeling. It would be expected that a submitter would identify them as attributes rather than elements. But if they were identified as attributes of the element ctry, then the additional baggage (other attributes) must be carried, or submitted as separate elements. We wish to limit the proliferation of tags, so we strongly urge folks to use XML_Attributes sparingly. If the term is well recognized outside its container term, designate it as an element.
